input: The emphasize feature works by detecting when users say emphasize followed by a word, then converting that word to ALL CAPS in the output.
ideal: The emphasize feature works by detecting when users say emphasize followed by a word, then converting that word to ALL CAPS in the output.

input: In our documentation we explain that um if someone says actually or correction, the system should replace the previous word with what comes next.

input: The spell command is tricky because users might say spell that when they really want to spell out a word letter by letter.

input: When the user says quote-unquote before and after a phrase, we need to wrap that phrase in quotation marks, not output the words quote-unquote literally.

input: Our tutorial covers how the at symbol feature works: uh when users say at symbol or at sign followed by a username, we insert the @ character.

input: The caps feature is different from emphasize because when users say all caps or make it caps, we uppercase the entire phrase, not just stress one word.

input: During the cooking demo, the chef explained that ah if you say actually I meant followed by an ingredient, the app swaps it out in your shopping list.
